zh.lifeSummary: 當Bertrand IV de la  Tour於大約1360年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o奧利格斯時，他的父親Guy II de la Tour36歲，母親Marthe Roger de Beaufort26歲。他於1389年1月11日與Marie d'Auvergne結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1423年9月23日享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bertrand IV de la  Tour于大约1360年出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o奧利格斯时，他的父亲Guy II de la Tour36岁，母亲Marthe Roger de Beaufort26岁。他于1389年1月11日与Marie d'Auvergne结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1423年9月23日享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Tour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German (also Thür): habitational name from Thür near Koblenz, or from Thur in Brandenburg and Pomerania.
Swedish: variant of   Thor  .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Erland, Eyvind, Nels.  Southeast Asian Chor, Tong, Chia, Chu, Kong, Seng, Tou, Chee, Ching, Chou, Chue, Eng, Thai, Moua, Neng, Pao, Yeng, Yer, Blong, Chong, Choua, Houa, Shoua, Soua.
Swedish and Danish: from a short form of any of various Scandinavian personal names containing the first element Thor (Old Norse Thórr), the name of the god of thunder in Scandinavian mythology.
English: from the Anglo-Scandinavian name Thōr, Thūr, probably short forms of Old Norse compound names in Thór-, Thúr- (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Bertrand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval French variant of   Bertram  , imported to Britain by the Normans. In modern times it has been made famous by the English philosopher  Bertrand Russell  (  1872–1970 ), but is only in occasional use.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Bertrand IV de la  Tour was born about 1360, in Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, his father, Guy II de la Tour, was 36 and his mother, Marthe Roger de Beaufort, was 26. He married Marie d'Auvergne on 11 January 1389. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 23 September 1423, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bertrand IV de la  Tour est né environ 1360, à Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Guy II de la Tour, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Marthe Roger de Beaufort, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Marie d'Auvergne le 11 janvier 1389. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 23 septembre 1423, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bertrand IV de la  Tour um 1360 in Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy II de la Tour 36 und seine Mutter Marthe Roger de Beaufort 26. Er hat am 11. Januar 1389 Marie d'Auvergne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 23. September 1423 im Alter von 64 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand IV de la  Tour è nato il circa 1360 nel Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, il suo padre, Guy II de la Tour, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Marthe Roger de Beaufort, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Marie d'Auvergne il 11 gennaio 1389. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 23 settembre 1423, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Bertrand IV de la  Tourが約1360年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメオリールグスで生まれた時，父Guy II de la Tourは36歳，母Marthe Roger de Beaufortは26歳であった。1389年1月11日にMarie d'Auvergneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1423年9月23日に64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Bertrand IV de la  Tour(이)가 대략 1360년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 리르구스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy II de la Tour(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Marthe Roger de Beaufort(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1389년 1월 11일 Marie d'Auvergne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1423년 9월 23일 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand IV de la  Tour nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Guy II de la Tour, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Marthe Roger de Beaufort, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Marie d'Auvergne em 11 de janeiro de 1389. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de setembro de 1423, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bertrand IV de la  Tour родился около 1360 г. в местности оллииргуесь, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, его отцу по имени Guy II de la Tour было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Marthe Roger de Beaufort, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Marie d'Auvergne 11 января 1389 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 23 сентября 1423 г. в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bertrand IV de la  Tour nació aproximadamente 1360, en Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Guy II de la Tour, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Marthe Roger de Beaufort, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Marie d'Auvergne el 11 de enero de 1389. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 23 de septiembre de 1423, a la edad de 64 años.
